message:
feat(build): add compile-time browser capability contract and exact-profile enforcement

Introduce compile-time gating for browser-incompatible surfaces so
downstream code can use #if ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE (etc.) instead of
relying solely on runtime checks.

- Add profile mutual-exclusion check in asx_config.h: #error fires when
  more than one ASX_PROFILE_* macro is defined simultaneously
- Introduce ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT and eight surface capability
  macros (ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES, ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER,
  ASX_HAS_BLOCKING_SURFACE, ASX_HAS_SERVER_SURFACE, ASX_HAS_GRPC_SURFACE,
  ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_SURFACE, ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE, ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE)
  all conditionally zeroed under ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER
- Add compile_time_profile_contract() test validating all 8 macros
  reflect profile selection correctly
- Update PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md to document new
  compile-time gating alongside existing runtime enforcement
- Close issue bd-djj0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -194,7 +194,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-aw69.6","title":"Validate R1 memory budget: static RAM/ROM footprint for Cortex-M4","description":"## STATUS: LAYOUT BUDGET REPORTING EXISTS — BARE-METAL R1 BUDGETS ARE NEW

## Context
The project ALREADY HAS layout/budget reporting for Linux-musl targets:
- tools/ci/generate_layout_budget_report.sh — generates per-target budget reports
- tools/ci/compute_layout_budget_delta.sh — computes deltas between runs
- run_embedded_matrix.sh invokes both automatically

This bead adds BARE-METAL memory budget validation, which is different:
- Linux-musl: measures shared library size and startup metrics
- Bare-metal: measures .text/.rodata/.data/.bss sections directly from linker map
- Bare-metal budgets are MUCH tighter (KB, not MB)

## Task
1. Create tools/ci/measure_baremetal_footprint.sh that:
   a. Accepts --profile, --target, --map-file, --run-id flags
   b. Parses .map file for .text/.rodata/.data/.bss sizes using awk/grep
   c. Computes total ROM (.text + .rodata) and total RAM (.data + .bss)
   d. Compares against R1 budget thresholds
   e. Reports in JSONL compatible with existing layout delta tooling
2. Set R1 budgets for both profiles on Cortex-M4:
   - FREESTANDING: ROM < 64KB, RAM < 8KB
   - EMBEDDED_ROUTER: ROM < 96KB, RAM < 16KB
3. Integrate with existing compute_layout_budget_delta.sh for regression detection
4. Document budget rationale in docs/EMBEDDED.md with per-section breakdown

## JSONL Output Format
{"ts":"...","target":"arm-m4","profile":"FREESTANDING","section":".text","size_bytes":28672,"budget_bytes":65536,"status":"pass"}
{"ts":"...","target":"arm-m4","profile":"FREESTANDING","section":".rodata","size_bytes":4096,"budget_bytes":65536,"status":"pass"}
{"ts":"...","target":"arm-m4","profile":"FREESTANDING","section":".data","size_bytes":512,"budget_bytes":8192,"status":"pass"}
{"ts":"...","target":"arm-m4","profile":"FREESTANDING","section":".bss","size_bytes":2048,"budget_bytes":8192,"status":"pass"}
{"ts":"...","target":"arm-m4","profile":"FREESTANDING","rom_total":32768,"rom_budget":65536,"ram_total":2560,"ram_budget":8192,"status":"pass"}

## Testing
1. Create tests/ci/test_measure_baremetal_footprint.sh:
   a. Generate a synthetic .map file with known section sizes
   b. Run measure_baremetal_footprint.sh against it
   c. Verify: reported sizes match expected values exactly
   d. Verify: budget pass/fail is correct for known sizes
   e. Test over-budget scenario: create .map with .text > 64KB, verify "fail" status
   f. Test edge case: exactly-at-budget (should pass)
   g. Verify: JSONL output is parseable by jq
   h. Verify: exit code is non-zero when any section exceeds budget

2. Integration test: after building a real bare-metal target (requires bd-aw69.5),
   run the footprint script and verify output matches readelf section sizes.

3. Regression test: run twice, feed both outputs to compute_layout_budget_delta.sh,
   verify delta is zero (deterministic builds).

## What Already Works (EXTEND, DON'T REPLACE)
- generate_layout_budget_report.sh (for musl targets)
- compute_layout_budget_delta.sh (for cross-run comparison)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bare-metal footprint measured reproducibly for both profiles on all 4 targets
- JSONL output per-section with summary line per target/profile
- Budget thresholds documented with rationale in docs/EMBEDDED.md
- Companion test script validates measurement logic with synthetic .map files
- Over-budget failures produce non-zero exit code and clear JSONL error detail
- JSON output compatible with existing compute_layout_budget_delta.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:13:50.789325787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:10:21.245600823Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:10:21.238544034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.6","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:13:50.789325787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.6","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:15:44.818607602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-aw69.7","title":"Integrate embedded targets into CI pipeline (GATE-EMBEDDED)","description":"## STATUS: EMBEDDED CI EXISTS — BARE-METAL GATE IS NEW

## Context
The CI ALREADY HAS:
- make ci-embedded-matrix: runs Linux-musl cross-builds
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh: full JSONL-logging matrix runner
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh: QEMU testing for Linux targets
- make check-ci includes ci-embedded-matrix

This bead adds the BARE-METAL gate (GATE-EMBEDDED-BAREMETAL) alongside the
existing Linux-musl embedded gate.

## Task
1. Create tools/ci/gate_embedded_baremetal.sh that:
   a. Runs make cross-baremetal-all
   b. Validates bare-metal linkage (no unresolved libc symbols)
   c. Runs measure_baremetal_footprint.sh and checks R1 budgets
   d. Runs QEMU bare-metal e2e tests (from bd-aw69.8)
   e. JSONL logging, graceful SKIP if toolchains missing

2. Add to make check-ci as an optional gate (after existing embedded-matrix)

3. DO NOT modify existing run_embedded_matrix.sh or ci-embedded-matrix target

## What Already Works (DO NOT TOUCH)
- make ci-embedded-matrix (Linux-musl)
- tools/ci/run_embedded_matrix.sh (Linux-musl)
- tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh (Linux user-mode)

## Acceptance Criteria
- gate_embedded_baremetal.sh exits 0 when all checks pass
- Graceful SKIP if arm-none-eabi-gcc or riscv toolchains missing
- Existing Linux-musl CI unaffected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:13:58.163837781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:19.953978795Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:39:19.953098470Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.7","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:13:58.163837781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.7","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:15:51.760011557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.7","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:04.244228748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-aw69.8","title":"QEMU e2e tests: run asupersync unit test subset on ARM and RISC-V targets","description":"## Context
The current QEMU setup (bd-aw69.3) only validates that trivial "return 0" binaries
run. This is necessary but insufficient — it proves QEMU works, not that asupersync
works on embedded targets. We need to run actual asupersync operations under
emulation to prove functional correctness on ARM and RISC-V.

## IMPORTANT: Existing Linux-musl QEMU Infrastructure
tools/ci/run_qemu_smoke.sh (395 lines) already provides a COMPLETE QEMU user-mode
test harness for Linux-musl targets (mipsel/armv7/aarch64). It includes:
- qemu-*-static binary detection and execution
- JSONL logging, timeout handling, artifact collection, trace digest
- CLI: --build-first, --dry-run, --strict, --timeout, --target

This bead creates COMPLEMENTARY bare-metal QEMU semihosting tests (ARM Cortex-M,
RISC-V), NOT a replacement for run_qemu_smoke.sh. The existing harness tests
Linux-musl targets; this bead tests bare-metal targets with semihosting.

## Background
Running the full 64-suite test battery under bare-metal QEMU is impractical: the tests
use host-specific features (printf for test output, malloc for CORE profile tests, etc.).
Instead, we need a curated subset of tests that:
- Use only FREESTANDING or EMBEDDED_ROUTER profile APIs
- Don't require host stdio (use semihosting for minimal output)
- Don't depend on real timers or threading
- Exercise the critical path: region lifecycle, task scheduling, obligation tracking

## Task
1. Create tests/embedded/embedded_smoke.c — a self-contained test program that:
   a. Provides custom asx_runtime_hooks (static buffer allocator, no-op log_sink,
      monotonic counter as logical_clock)
   b. Opens a region, spawns tasks, runs scheduler, drains region
   c. Verifies task states, obligation states, quiescence
   d. Tests channel operations (EMBEDDED_ROUTER profile only, ifdef-guarded)
   e. Reports pass/fail via semihosting exit code (0 = pass, non-zero = fail count)
   f. Optionally emits minimal test log via semihosting SYS_WRITE0

2. Create tests/embedded/embedded_channel_smoke.c — channel-specific tests for
   EMBEDDED_ROUTER profile:
   a. Channel create, reserve, send, recv, close
   b. Backpressure (full channel returns correct error)
   c. Fairness (round-robin recv across multiple senders)

3. Create tools/ci/run_qemu_e2e.sh that:
   a. Cross-compiles embedded_smoke.c + libasx.a for each bare-metal target
   b. Runs under QEMU with semihosting (qemu-system-arm -semihosting, etc.)
   c. Captures exit code and semihosting output
   d. Reports per-target results with JSONL logging
   e. Runs EMBEDDED_ROUTER tests on targets that support it
   f. Uses same JSONL format conventions as existing run_qemu_smoke.sh for consistency

4. JSONL Log Format (consistent with run_qemu_smoke.sh):
   {"ts":"...","test":"embedded_smoke","target":"arm-m4-free","status":"pass","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":1200}
   {"ts":"...","test":"embedded_channel_smoke","target":"arm-m4-router","status":"pass","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":1800}

5. Test the test: create tests/embedded/test_hooks_minimal.c that unit-tests the
   custom hooks implementation (static allocator, counter clock) to ensure the test
   infrastructure itself is correct.

## Integration with Existing Infrastructure
- run_qemu_e2e.sh should REUSE tools/ci/run_qemu_baremetal.sh (from bd-aw69.3) for
  the actual QEMU semihosting invocation — do NOT duplicate QEMU launch logic
- run_qemu_e2e.sh handles: cross-compilation, test selection, result aggregation
- run_qemu_baremetal.sh handles: QEMU binary selection, semihosting flags, timeout, exit code capture
- Both scripts follow the same CLI conventions as run_qemu_smoke.sh
  (--dry-run, --strict, --timeout, --target)
- JSONL output should be compatible with the same log aggregation tooling
- The CI gate (bd-aw69.7) should run BOTH run_qemu_smoke.sh (Linux-musl) AND
  run_qemu_e2e.sh (bare-metal) for comprehensive coverage

## Static Allocator Design
The FREESTANDING profile needs a static buffer allocator for hooks:
- Pre-allocated uint8_t buffer[8192] (configurable)
- Bump allocator with alignment support
- realloc via copy-to-new-offset (no real realloc needed)
- free is a no-op (bump allocator doesn't reclaim)
- This is intentionally simple — good enough for test scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- embedded_smoke.c passes on all 4 FREESTANDING targets (arm-m4, arm-m0, riscv32, riscv64)
- embedded_channel_smoke.c passes on all 4 EMBEDDED_ROUTER targets
- All 8 test runs produce exit code 0 under QEMU semihosting
- JSONL log output compatible with existing run_qemu_smoke.sh format
- Static allocator is tested independently
- Test exercises at minimum: region open/close, task spawn/schedule/complete,
  obligation reserve/commit, quiescence check, cleanup callback

## Why This Matters
The existing run_qemu_smoke.sh validates Linux-musl targets, which still depend on
libc. Bare-metal semihosting tests prove that asupersync's FREESTANDING and
EMBEDDED_ROUTER profiles truly work without ANY OS or libc — the core value
proposition of the ANSI C port for embedded deployment.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:29:25.299905528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:37:01.198875230Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:37:01.196321167Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.8","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:29:25.299905528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.8","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:29:39.933558552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-aw69.8","depends_on_id":"bd-aw69.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:29:56.999052265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-exsk","title":"Extend browser-profile focused coverage to service, transport, and remote families","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:52:14.317611137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:56:10.891755063Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:56:10.891137184Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-fjer","title":"Fix combinator deadline leak and quorum drain semantics","description":"Three semantic bugs in combinators: (1) timeout/retry_timeout never disarm deadlines from timer wheel after completing, leaking timer resources; (2) quorum combinator marks loser branches done=1 without calling branch_cancel_after_final_poll(), violating the cancellation contract that all other combinators (race, select) honor; (3) race_timeout can double-drain losers when timeout and winner declaration coincide.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:05:18.792349514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:08:25.444376306Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:08:25.443695968Z","close_reason":"Fixed all three combinator semantic bugs: deadline disarm on timeout/retry_timeout, quorum drain with cooperative final poll, race_timeout winner-vs-timeout priority. 134 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["combinator","correctness"]}
 {"id":"bd-hwb","title":"EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99","description":"Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155834562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:59:25.155810537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","safety","semantics"]}

@@ -267,15 +267,15 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 
 | Upstream contract | Current ANSI C equivalent | State | Evidence / gap |
 |---|---|---|---|
-| `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, but there is still no compile-time browser subprofile split or mutual-exclusion model because the C port only exposes one browser mode today |
-| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` now fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, but there is still no compile-time `native-runtime`-style feature gate model |
-| `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Native I/O blocked under browser boundary | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_IO_DRIVER` is denied in browser mode, but there is no separate browser-IO feature flag or profile-minimal compile check |
+| `wasm32` must choose exactly one canonical browser profile | `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER` exists in `include/asx/asx_config.h`, `src/runtime/profile_compat.c`, the explicit `build-browser` / `PROFILE=BROWSER` lane, and the focused `test-browser-focused` CI/test lane in `Makefile` | `partial` | The browser build/test surface is now explicit and CI-covered, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now hard-fails multiple `ASX_PROFILE_*` selections while declaring `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT == 1`, but there is still no compile-time browser subprofile split because the C port only exposes one browser mode today |
+| `native-runtime` forbidden on browser builds | Browser boundary gates native surfaces and compile-time capability macros mark them unavailable | `partial` | `include/asx/runtime/browser_boundary.h` fail-closes filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus the shipped `server`/`grpc`/`messaging` families, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES == 0` under browser builds, but native-only APIs are still publicly declared rather than removed from the browser umbrella surface |
+| `browser-io` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Native I/O blocked under browser boundary and compile-time capability macros | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_IO_DRIVER` is denied in browser mode and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER == 0`, but there is still no separate browser-IO feature flag or profile-minimal compile check |
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | No separate browser trace feature | `gap` | The C tree has browser diagnostics and trace support, but no compile-time browser trace subprofile split |
 | `cli` unsupported on browser builds | No `cli` module in C | `gap` | The C port currently lacks a public CLI family entirely, so there is no feature-gated fail-closed browser rule to enforce |
 | `io-uring` unsupported on browser builds | No io_uring feature surface in C | `mapped-by-absence` | No equivalent feature exists yet; once native reactor features expand, browser incompatibility must become explicit |
-| `tls`, `tls-native-roots`, `tls-webpki-roots` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped TLS family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_TLS` is now fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning TLS entry points, but there is still no compile-time TLS feature split |
-| `sqlite`, `postgres`, `mysql` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped database family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_DATABASE` is now fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning database entry points, but there is still no compile-time database feature split |
-| `kafka` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped messaging/broker family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_MESSAGING` is now fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning messaging entry points, but there is still no compile-time broker feature split |
+| `tls`, `tls-native-roots`, `tls-webpki-roots` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped TLS family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_TLS` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning TLS entry points, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, but there is still no sub-feature split for trust-root variants |
+| `sqlite`, `postgres`, `mysql` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped database family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_DATABASE` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning database entry points, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, but there is still no backend-specific compile-time split |
+| `kafka` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped messaging/broker family in browser mode | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_MESSAGING` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning messaging entry points, and `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, but there is still no broker-specific compile-time split |
 | Native-only modules (`fs`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `process`, `server`, `signal`) excluded from wasm32 | Browser boundary denies the currently shipped native-facing families | `partial` | The boundary now covers filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus explicit `server`/`grpc`/`messaging`/`tls`/`database` surfaces, and CI now runs focused browser-profile suites over shipped browser-safe `actor`/`sync`/`bytes`/`encoding`/`decoding`/`stream`/`security`/`plan`/`cx`/`link`/`app`/`console`/`tracing_compat`/`evidence`/`monitor`/`record`/`migration`/`raptorq`/`spork` plus `net`/`http`/`web`/`websocket`/`quic`/`distributed`/`pipe` and `service`/`transport`/`remote`; remaining debt is compile-time feature gating plus future higher-surface matrix growth |
 
 ### 8.1 Missing Fail-Closed Checks Before Parity Claims
@@ -285,9 +285,9 @@ can honestly claim equivalence with the upstream feature/platform matrix:
 
 1. Compile-time mutual exclusion for browser-specific subprofiles if the C port
    adopts more than one browser mode.
-2. Explicit compile-time gating for browser-incompatible families rather than
-   relying only on the runtime fail-closed boundary and browser-focused test
-   lanes.
+2. Explicit compile-time removal of browser-incompatible APIs from the public
+   browser umbrella/header surface rather than relying on capability macros plus
+   runtime fail-closed boundary and browser-focused test lanes.
 3. A single compatibility matrix that states which combinations are supported,
    denied, or currently unimplemented rather than leaving absence implicit.

@@ -41,13 +41,54 @@
  * Profile selection (exactly one must be defined at compile time).
  * If none is defined, ASX_PROFILE_CORE is assumed.
  */
-#if !defined(ASX_PROFILE_CORE) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32) &&    \
-    !defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER) &&                 \
-    !defined(ASX_PROFILE_HFT) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_AUTOMOTIVE) &&                               \
+#if (defined(ASX_PROFILE_CORE) + defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX) + defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32) +        \
+     defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING) + defined(ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER) +                   \
+     defined(ASX_PROFILE_HFT) + defined(ASX_PROFILE_AUTOMOTIVE) + defined(ASX_PROFILE_PARALLEL) + \
+     defined(ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER)) > 1
+#error "Exactly one ASX_PROFILE_* macro may be defined per build"
+#elif !defined(ASX_PROFILE_CORE) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32) &&  \
+    !defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_EMBEDDED_ROUTER) &&                \
+    !defined(ASX_PROFILE_HFT) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_AUTOMOTIVE) &&                              \
     !defined(ASX_PROFILE_PARALLEL) && !defined(ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER)
 #define ASX_PROFILE_CORE
 #endif
 
+/* -------------------------------------------------------------------
+ * Compile-time profile capability contract
+ *
+ * These macros expose the public build contract to downstream code so
+ * browser-incompatible surfaces can be gated in preprocessor space as
+ * well as at runtime. The current C port still exposes a single browser
+ * mode, so subprofile counts remain explicit rather than inferred.
+ * ------------------------------------------------------------------- */
+
+#define ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MODE_COUNT 1
+
+#if defined(ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER)
+#define ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES 0
+#define ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER 0
+#define ASX_HAS_BLOCKING_SURFACE 0
+#define ASX_HAS_SERVER_SURFACE 0
+#define ASX_HAS_GRPC_SURFACE 0
+#define ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_SURFACE 0
+#define ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE 0
+#define ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE 0
+#else
+#define ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES 1
+#define ASX_HAS_NATIVE_IO_DRIVER 1
+#define ASX_HAS_BLOCKING_SURFACE 1
+#define ASX_HAS_SERVER_SURFACE 1

+#define ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE 1

+
+/* Browser trace remains a single-mode contract today: tracing is available,
+ * but there is no minimal-vs-extended browser trace subprofile split yet. */
+#define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE 1
+#define ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE_SUBPROFILE_SPLIT 0
+
 /* Debug mode: enables ghost monitors (borrow ledger, protocol, linearity, determinism) */
 #if !defined(ASX_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
 #define ASX_DEBUG 1
